checks.comment: No variability classification in Gaia DR3 vari tables. At P = 444 d, low-amplitude ellipsoidal modulation may be present but below Gaia detection threshold.  The high eccentricity (e = 0.583) would produce stronger tidal modulation near periastron.

No bright neighbour within 5 arcsec in Gaia DR3.  At G = 6.47 (naked eye brightness), the source is overwhelmingly dominant.  The moderately elevated RUWE (5.4) is attributed to the orbital photo-centre motion, not blending.

No SIMBAD HD/HIP/HR identifier found in the workspace.  At G = 6.47 this is unusual for such a bright star.  It may have a Tycho-2 designation or be in the southern Bright Star Catalogue under a different identifier.  No prior literature identifies a compact companion.

checks.eRASS: Not yet public at this position
checks.comment: No ROSAT or XMM detection.  At b = -24 deg, Galactic absorption is moderate.  The absence of X-ray emission is consistent with a quiescent (non-accreting) BH in a detached system with e = 0.583 and P = 444 d.

At l = 345.4 deg, b = -24.1 deg, d = 680 pc, z = -0.28 kpc below the plane.  This is in the Telescopium/Pavo region of the southern sky.  The moderate Galactic latitude and distance suggest thin or thick disk membership. RV = -9.8 +/- 2.5 km/s is not extreme.

summary: All archival veto checks passed.  SIMBAD cross-ID should be confirmed for a bright star at G = 6.47.
